title: MicroRNAs may reveal type 1 diabetes
author: Nyrönen, Tommi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: MicroRNAs are short RNA strands, of which more than 2,300 have been identified in humans. Their abnormal expression contributes to the development of many diseases, such as cardiovascular and immunological diseases and cancer. Researchers at the University of Turku discovered a microRNA that may be an early indicator of the risk of developing type 1 diabetes.
